---
page-layout: full
title: Melanie Medina López, PhD
---

:::::::::: columns
::::::: {.column width="29%"}
:::::: about-left
::: about-photo
![](images/MedinaLopez_Melanie_cropped.jpg){width="210"}
:::

::: about-links
[<i class="bi bi-github" style="font-size: 1.5rem;"></i>](https://github.com/melaniemml) [<i class="bi bi-linkedin" style="font-size: 1.5rem;"></i>](https://www.linkedin.com/in/melanie-medina-lopez-893a81158/) [<i class="bi bi-envelope-at-fill" style="font-size: 1.5rem;"></i>](mailto:mkm6528@psu.edu)
:::

::: about-button
<a href="images/26Mar_CV_MedinaLopez.pdf" class="btn btn-secondary" role="button" target="_blank"> View CV </a>
:::
::::::
:::::::

::: {.column width="2%"}
:::

::: {.column width="69%"}
## About

I'm a microbial ecologists fascinated by how microbes influence agroecosystems. Currently, I am a postdoctoral scholar in the Roman-Reyna Lab at Penn State, where I study plant microbiomes and the microbial genomics of plant pathogens. In my previous work I have studied diverse agricultural systems to understand the impact of management and soil legacy on microbial communities and plant health.
:::
::::::::::
